interactions.summary: Sneako asks about the late Dugin conversation; Jiang says he wanted to ask how Dugin reconciles Moscow as Third Rome with earlier Christian views of Rome as Antichrist.
Dave Smith joins, checks audio, and jokes that Trump has declared victory so the discussion may already be over.
interactions.speaker_attribution: SPEAKER_01 appears to be Dave Smith.

interactions.summary: Sneako asks what Trump said; Dave explains that Maria Bartiromo reported Trump declaring the war over and treats it as Trumpian performative reality-making.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_01 is Dave Smith from his entrance through the Trump victory discussion.

claims.claim: Jiang identifies an unresolved religious-political problem in Dugin's Third Rome framing: Rome can be imagined as both Christian imperial inheritance and Antichristic beast system.
Jiang thinks Orthodox-Catholic differences may be reconcilable, while Protestant-Orthodox differences are harder to reconcile.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang asks how Moscow can become Third Rome when early Christians cast Rome as the beast system.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns the Dugin question from geopolitics into a contradiction inside Christian imperial inheritance.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Moscow's claimed inheritance of Rome's Christian imperial role, which Jiang tests against Protestant and early Christian suspicion of Rome.

chronology_notes.note: Dave references a Trump interview reported for airing at 6 a.m. after 2026-04-15, where Trump was said to declare the war over.
